threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still check your phone for Tube times instead of just walking to the nearest station like everyone else. It’s a whole different vibe here.
Caught the N37 last night, and a group of teens had an impromptu rap battle on the upper deck. Even the driver was nodding along. Only in Brixton.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The day I realized the local Turkish bakery runs out of Simit by 10 AM, but the pop-up coffee shop is always packed at noon. Classic Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: In Friedrichshain, there’s a hipster café that serves beer-flavored ice cream. You wouldn’t find that combo anywhere else, trust me.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s from here when they know the best taco truck is parked by the graffiti wall, not the fancy food truck rally.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Yesterday, I watched a bus driver calmly coax a lost dog off the tracks at Civic Center—only in SF, right? Muni's unpredictable moments keep us on our toes.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think all the buildings are just pretty facades; locals know the real life happens in the courtyards, where you'll find the best chats and the odd stray cat.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by their tendency to insist on ordering 'café con leche' instead of the local 'café amb llet' – an easy tell in Gràcia cafes.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think the 30-minute wait for a bus is outrageous. We're a '15 minutes away' type of people, even when it takes an hour.
Caught a night bus last week, driver was blasting reggae. Everyone was vibing until a rat darted in, and we all froze mid-sway.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw kids playing football in the street while their parents chatted over coffee at the corner kiosk. Pure Neukölln chaos.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin do you see DJs spinning records while it snows, creating a surreal winter rave vibe on the East Side Gallery.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a local by their reaction to a siren – they hardly flinch, while newcomers whip around, startled like deer in headlights.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy in a tuxedo trying to coax his pet iguana off the Muni steps at Civic Center, while the bus just sat there. Classic San Francisco chaos.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think ‘La Rambla’ is the heart of Barcelona, but really, it’s the little plazas in Gràcia where life buzzes with locals and tapas flow.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by the way they struggle with 'cava' vs. 'champagne' at the local bar—it's always the foreigner asking for a bubbly 'on the rocks.'
